---
title: "Corvus Star Phase 3 Implementation Backlog"
date: 2026-03-09
status: completed
---

# Corvus Star Phase 3 Implementation Backlog

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This backlog operationalizes Phase 3 from `docs/architecture/WOVEN_SKILL_CONSOLIDATION_BLUEPRINT.qmd`.
> Phase 3 breaks daemon monoliths into skill stages.
> Muninn may remain a runtime identity, but it cannot remain a business-logic owner.

## Phase 3 Mission

Phase 3 exists to remove exclusive behavior from daemon loops.

The required outcomes are:

1. Hunt, memory, validation, and promotion each gain explicit stage contracts.
2. The ravens autonomous cycle is composed from runtime-dispatched stages rather than embedded inside `MuninnHeart`.
3. Legacy daemon entrypoints become transition adapters instead of operational authorities.
4. Stage outcomes are recorded in Hall-backed records and explicit projections instead of ad hoc trace files or hidden ledgers.
5. The autonomous cycle becomes testable at the stage level without booting a long-lived daemon.

## Phase 3 Exit Criteria

- No active Muninn or ravens loop file owns hunt selection, validation policy, or promotion side effects as exclusive inline behavior.
- A canonical cycle weave or equivalent runtime composition path invokes named hunt, memory, validation, and promotion stages.
- `MuninnHeart` is removed or reduced to a thin transitional composition wrapper with no exclusive business rules.
- `MuninnHunter`, `MuninnMemory`, `MuninnCrucible`, and promotion logic each expose contract-backed stage entrypoints with targeted tests.
- Legacy daemon commands return lifecycle state only and delegate execution to the runtime or explicit stage adapters.
- Stage outcomes persist through Hall-backed validation, observation, and bead state records; `tech_debt_ledger.json`, `.agents/traces`, and other legacy artifacts remain compatibility-only if still present.

## Execution Status

Current Phase 3 state:

- Phase 1 completed on 2026-03-09
- Phase 2 completed on 2026-03-09
- `weave:ravens` is still a lifecycle wrapper, not a stage-composed cycle executor
- `CS-P3-00` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P3-01` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P3-02` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P3-03` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P3-04` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P3-05` closed on 2026-03-09
- `MuninnMemory` is now the Hall-backed observation surface, with trace files reduced to compatibility mirrors
- `MuninnCrucible` now exposes a Hall-backed validation stage result instead of a daemon-local validation gate
- `MuninnPromotion` now owns rollback, watcher acceptance, trace emission, and bead finalization or blocking
- Post-phase kernel cleanup removed the remaining live websocket daemon and PID ownership assumptions from `CortexLink`, the gateway bridge, `MuninnHeart`, and `ravens start`, replacing them with one-shot kernel dispatch and sweep semantics
- Phase 3 completed on 2026-03-09
- Current next ticket: none

## Ticket Index

| ID | Status | Priority | Ticket | Depends On | Primary Surfaces |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `CS-P3-00` | Closed | P0 | Ravens Cycle Stage Contract Freeze | Phase 2 complete | `runtime/contracts.ts`, `runtime/bootstrap.ts`, `MuninnHeart`, stage dispatch tests |
| `CS-P3-01` | Closed | P0 | Hunt Stage Extraction | `CS-P3-00` | `muninn_hunter.py`, `coordinator.py`, Norn/bead selection, `tech_debt_ledger.json` |
| `CS-P3-02` | Closed | P1 | Memory and Observation Stage Extraction | `CS-P3-00` | `muninn_memory.py`, behavioral pulse, Hall observations, `.agents/traces` |
| `CS-P3-03` | Closed | P0 | Crucible Validation Stage Extraction | `CS-P3-00` | `muninn_crucible.py`, validation contract, fishtest and benchmark surfaces |
| `CS-P3-04` | Closed | P1 | Promotion Stage Extraction | `CS-P3-01`, `CS-P3-03` | `MuninnHeart`, `stability.py`, bead lifecycle, apply and rollback behavior |
| `CS-P3-05` | Closed | P1 | Daemon Adapter Collapse | `CS-P3-01`, `CS-P3-02`, `CS-P3-03`, `CS-P3-04` | `main_loop.py`, `muninn.py`, `repo_spoke.py`, `runtime/adapters.ts`, `src/cstar/core/daemon.py` |

## Critical Path

The critical path is:

`CS-P3-00 -> CS-P3-01 -> CS-P3-03 -> CS-P3-04 -> CS-P3-05`

`CS-P3-02` should begin once stage contracts are frozen and land before daemon adapter collapse.

## Ticket Details

## `CS-P3-00` Ravens Cycle Stage Contract Freeze

### Objective

Freeze the stage contract and cycle composition boundary before extracting legacy Muninn behavior.

### Files

- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/bootstrap.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/dispatcher.ts`
- `src/sentinel/muninn_heart.py`
- New or updated tests for stage dispatch and cycle composition

### Change Set

- Define canonical invocation and result envelopes for hunt, memory, validation, and promotion stages.
- Define a runtime composition contract for one ravens cycle so the cycle can be executed without a daemon loop owning all state.
- Ensure stage envelopes carry bead ids, target identity, validation ids, and Hall observation metadata instead of ad hoc dictionaries and booleans.
- Register stage-capable runtime entrypoints so Phase 3 extraction can proceed incrementally without inventing new side channels.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Each stage can be dispatched through a stable runtime id.
- Stage results are structured and typed; they do not depend on inline `dict` shape guessing or boolean-only success flags.
- `MuninnHeart` can consume the frozen stage envelopes without inventing private payload formats.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_runtime_dispatch.test.ts tests/unit/test_ravens_lifecycle.test.ts
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/contracts/test_muninn.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- TypeScript and Python now share an explicit ravens-cycle and ravens-stage contract surface.
- The runtime now registers `weave:ravens-cycle` plus stable `weave:ravens-memory`, `weave:ravens-hunt`, `weave:ravens-validate`, and `weave:ravens-promote` contract entrypoints.
- `MuninnHeart` now emits a structured cycle result with stage summaries, target identity, Hall observation references, and validation ids while preserving the legacy boolean wrapper.
- A dedicated ravens-cycle Python entrypoint now exists for runtime composition without going through the long-lived daemon wrapper.

## `CS-P3-01` Hunt Stage Extraction

### Objective

Extract target discovery and mission selection into a canonical hunt stage over Hall-backed work units.

### Files

- `src/sentinel/muninn_hunter.py`
- `src/sentinel/coordinator.py`
- `src/core/norn_coordinator.py`
- `src/sentinel/wardens/norn.py`
- `src/core/engine/bead_ledger.py`
- New or updated tests for hunt stage selection

### Change Set

- Move mission selection out of `MuninnHeart` into a dedicated hunt stage.
- Replace `tech_debt_ledger.json` and legacy breach sorting as active authority with bead or Hall-backed target selection.
- Normalize current mission payloads so a hunt result always returns canonical target identity, rationale, baseline scores, and acceptance criteria.
- Keep `tech_debt_ledger.json` only as a compatibility projection or explicit migration input if any legacy readers still require it.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No active autonomous cycle path reads `.agents/tech_debt_ledger.json` as canonical mission authority.
- Hunt results are bead-backed or explicitly Hall-backed and traceable to canonical records.
- `MissionCoordinator` and `MuninnHunter` become thin stage adapters or retire.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/contracts/test_muninn.py tests/unit/test_bead_ledger.py tests/unit/test_norn_coordinator_empire.py tests/empire_tests/test_norn_empire.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `MissionCoordinator` now resolves canonical bead-backed missions before any compatibility fallback.
- `MuninnHeart` now claims or selects missions from the sovereign bead ledger instead of treating `tech_debt_ledger.json` as live authority.
- `MuninnHunter` now scans the Hall-backed bead queue instead of loading legacy ledger targets.
- Legacy `tech_debt_ledger.json` selection remains available only as an explicit compatibility path when no canonical bead is available.

## `CS-P3-02` Memory and Observation Stage Extraction

### Objective

Move behavioral pulse, trace capture, and observation sync out of daemon-owned memory helpers and into Hall-backed stage behavior.

### Files

- `src/sentinel/muninn_memory.py`
- `src/sentinel/muninn_heart.py`
- `src/core/engine/hall_schema.py`
- `src/node/core/state.ts`
- `src/sentinel/stability.py`
- New or updated tests for memory and observation stage behavior

### Change Set

- Extract the behavioral pulse and intent-integrity sync into a dedicated memory or observation stage.
- Replace `.agents/traces` as an authoritative outcome sink with Hall-backed skill observations, validation records, or explicit compatibility projections.
- Record cycle traces, pulse outcomes, and watcher-related metadata through Hall-backed state first.
- Keep `.agents/traces` and `.agents/sprt_ledger.json` only as compatibility artifacts if they must remain during transition.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No active cycle path persists authoritative mission outcomes only to `.agents/traces`.
- `MuninnMemory` no longer owns canonical trajectory persistence.
- Intent-integrity updates remain Hall-first and only mirror compatibility projections where required.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_state_registry_projection.test.ts
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/contracts/test_muninn.py tests/unit/test_hall_schema.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `MuninnMemory` now writes stage observations and trajectory traces into Hall-backed skill observations.
- `.agents/traces` now exists only as a compatibility projection mirror and no longer as the sole mission outcome sink.
- Intent-integrity sync from `sprt_ledger.json` now updates Hall first and mirrors `sovereign_state.json` only for compatibility.
- `MuninnHeart` now delegates observation persistence and SPRT sync to `MuninnMemory` instead of owning those writes directly.

## `CS-P3-03` Crucible Validation Stage Extraction

### Objective

Turn Muninn validation into an explicit stage contract and remove validation policy from daemon orchestration.

### Files

- `src/sentinel/muninn_crucible.py`
- `src/core/engine/validation_result.py`
- `sterileAgent/fishtest.py`
- `src/tools/benchmark_engine.py`
- New or updated tests for validation stage pass, fail, and timeout paths

### Change Set

- Expose Crucible verification through a canonical validation stage that returns `ValidationResult` rather than boolean-only success.
- Split candidate generation helpers from verdict emission or mark them as transitional forge adapters so validation owns validation only.
- Attach benchmark, gauntlet, Crucible, and SPRT data to one stage result envelope and Hall-backed validation path.
- Keep `verify_fix()` only as a compatibility wrapper until all callers consume structured validation results.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No active promotion path depends on `verify_fix()` as the authoritative gate.
- Validation stage results include validation id, verdict, blocking reasons, and traceable test metadata.
- Crucible and fishtest outputs are visible through one contract-backed stage surface.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/contracts/test_muninn.py tests/crucible/test_muninn.py tests/unit/test_validation_result.py tests/empire_tests/test_fishtest_empire.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `MuninnCrucible` now exposes a contract-backed validation stage that returns a `RavensStageResult` with validation ids, verdict metadata, and target identity.
- Candidate preparation is now separated into an explicit transitional forge adapter so validation policy can be consumed without re-embedding generation logic in `MuninnHeart`.
- Validation success and failure now persist through Hall-backed validation runs and stage observations instead of boolean-only daemon-local control flow.

## `CS-P3-04` Promotion Stage Extraction

### Objective

Lift patch application, rollback, watcher updates, and bead state transitions out of `MuninnHeart` and into an explicit promotion stage.

### Files

- `src/sentinel/muninn_heart.py`
- `src/sentinel/stability.py`
- `src/core/engine/bead_ledger.py`
- `src/core/engine/forge_candidate.py`
- `src/core/norn_coordinator.py`
- New or updated tests for promotion accept, reject, and rollback behavior

### Change Set

- Define a promotion stage that consumes a candidate, target identity, and validation result and emits explicit promotion outcome metadata.
- Move file apply, rollback, watcher lock and failure recording, and bead lifecycle transitions out of `MuninnHeart`.
- Require promotion to record Hall-backed outcome metadata and respect the canonical bead lifecycle.
- Ensure failed promotion paths roll back cleanly and preserve explicit failure reasons for later review.

### Acceptance Criteria

- `MuninnHeart` no longer calls file apply, rollback, or watcher side effects directly.
- Promotion cannot resolve or close work without validation-backed evidence and canonical lifecycle transitions.
- Promotion outcomes are reproducible in tests without booting the daemon loop.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/contracts/test_muninn.py tests/unit/test_bead_ledger.py tests/unit/test_evolve_skill.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `MuninnPromotion` now owns rollback behavior, watcher acceptance, trace emission, and canonical bead finalization or blocking outcomes.
- Successful autonomous promotion now resolves beads only through validation-linked Hall evidence, while failed promotion paths roll back and move work to explicit blocked review state instead of leaving it claimed.
- `MuninnHeart` now composes promotion as a stage adapter and no longer performs rollback, watcher failure handling, or bead lifecycle transitions inline.

## `CS-P3-05` Daemon Adapter Collapse

### Objective

Reduce the remaining daemon and wrapper files to lifecycle adapters over the stage-composed runtime.

### Files

- `src/sentinel/main_loop.py`
- `src/sentinel/muninn.py`
- `src/sentinel/repo_spoke.py`
- `src/node/core/runtime/adapters.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/bootstrap.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/ravens.ts`
- `src/cstar/core/daemon.py`
- New or updated lifecycle and cycle delegation tests

### Change Set

- Add a canonical ravens-cycle runtime entrypoint that composes the extracted stages.
- Make `weave:ravens` responsible only for lifecycle and transition behavior around that cycle entrypoint.
- Reduce `main_loop.py`, `muninn.py`, `repo_spoke.py`, and `src/cstar/core/daemon.py` to explicit compatibility wrappers or retire them.
- Keep cadence, locking, and process management only where compatibility still requires them; remove all exclusive business logic.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No daemon wrapper owns hunt selection, memory recording, validation policy, or promotion side effects.
- `ravens` command surfaces delegate to runtime stage composition instead of launching an opaque monolith.
- One-cycle execution can be tested directly through runtime composition without spawning the long-lived daemon.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsx --test tests/unit/test_ravens_lifecycle.test.ts tests/unit/test_runtime_dispatch.test.ts
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/contracts/test_main_loop.py tests/contracts/test_muninn.py -q
```

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented outcomes:

- `weave:ravens` now remains a lifecycle wrapper but delegates one-cycle execution to `weave:ravens-cycle`, and the CLI exposes that composed path directly through `ravens cycle`.
- `Muninn`, `repo_spoke.py`, and `ravens_cycle.py` now share one runtime-composed cycle entrypoint instead of constructing separate daemon-local execution paths.
- The legacy websocket daemon remains a compatibility bridge, but its remaining Muninn-facing verification path now delegates to canonical Crucible surfaces rather than a nonexistent private wrapper.
